item.title: Why Does The Internet Think Hideo Kojima And Geoff Keighley Got ‘Divorced’?
item.content:encoded:
Geoff Keighley and Hideo Kojima have been besties ever since 2001, and their unbreakable, long-standing bond—that Naruto and Sasuke-esque affection for one another—is an oft-memed topic among the gaming community. However, over the past week, the internet has gotten a little freakier about Kojima and Keighley’s affinity for one another, because rumors are flying that our two favorite gaming dads are getting a “divorce.”

While most have taken this as an opportunity to post silly break-up memes and, in at least one instance, some Kojima x Keighley fanfics, some are digging a little deeper into the two men’s supposed falling out—arguably, a bit too deep. Most of the posts over the past few days seem to have stemmed from this post on June 5, which states that “Kojima has interacted a total of ZERO (0) times with Geoff Keighley’s tweets since the infamous Game Awards gala where Expedition 33 took home GOTY.”

Whether or not that’s true is, as far as the internet is concerned, completely beside the point, because both Keighley and Kojima have clearly decided to have a bit of fun with the charade. For instance, The Washington Post’s Gene Park posted a picture of himself with Kojima on the same day, with a quote reply stating “plz don’t show this to geoff keighley.” Kojima instantly decided to retweet it… twice.

diabolical 😭😭😭 https://t.co/xSVW3lqVhd pic.twitter.com/3zM6nOPhmA

— Gene Park (@GenePark) June 7, 2026

Geoff has been having some fun with it too, and has decided to start replying to a bunch of Kojima’s posts as if he’s cosplaying as someone obsessively longing to get back in touch with an old flame. The internet has yet to pick a side in the divorce, although I have seen several replies instructing Geoff to “never give up” on his one true love. 

using 🫦 here would’ve been way more honest

— Dinostoned (@Undead_Leader) June 7, 2026

So, are Kojima and Geoff actually beefing? Did Geoff announcing that Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 won GOTY instead of Death Stranding 2 really drive a wedge between the two besties? Or was it that homewrecker Miss Piggy? Obviously not, otherwise I doubt they’d be making such a joke out of it. But hey, at least we got some all-timer X posts out of all this.

it's almost been six months since my fathers (hideo kojima and geoff keighley) divorced… pic.twitter.com/KPUBpUDhqC

— excruciate (@wiithteeth) June 5, 2026

To heal I put Geoff and kojima on tomodachi island pic.twitter.com/JnbufRpC8k

— Ch4rl13 (@milktst) June 7, 2026

who tf am I supposed to talk to about the Geoff keighley Kojima divorce. this is a level of online that my friends just do not understand.

— phase (@phaseknight_) June 7, 2026

I just hope they make up in time for the 2026 Game Awards. I mean, it really wouldn’t feel the same without the two awkwardly shuffling around each other as Geoff stares into Kojima’s eyes while his translator third-wheels it on stage.



https://kotaku.com/hideo-kojima-geoff-keighley-friendship-divorce-2000704057
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Xbox Game Pass Lost “Millions of Subscribers” Due to 50% Price Hike, Admits Chief Strategy Officer
item.content:encoded:
Shortly before he was on his way out, one of the final decisions made by Phil Spencer and his regime at Xbox was to give Xbox Game Pass a massive 50% price hike. Users rushed to cancel their subscriptions so quickly and in such high droves in response that it crashed the cancel page on Xbox's website, so it's no surprise to hear the new strategy chief Matthew Ball admit that the company lost "millions" of subscribers due to the increase.

This comes from Ball while speaking at The Game Business Live during Summer Game Fest 2026, first shared by The Game Business partner Geoff Keighley. Ball doesn't say just how many subscribers, but it's not surprising in the least that a massive chunk decided they didn't want to pay $30 a month for Game Pass Ultimate.

Of course, the new regime under Asha Sharma has since clawed back that price hike somewhat, which also significantly changed the service with its removal of new Call of Duty games. But it's not like lowering the price suddenly made those millions of players who probably enjoyed not seeing Game Pass on their monthly bills, turned around and signed up again.

Sharma has made it clear she is personally leading the charge to make Xbox a competitive platform again, and that means making Xbox hardware viable for players to consider again. Exclusive games are part of that strategy, as we officially learned during the Xbox Games Showcase, and Ball also added further clarification on Xbox's strategy with exclusives going forward.

After Matt Booty said future exclusives will be chosen on a "case-by-case" basis and that multiplayer games would remain multiplatform, Ball reaffirmed that, and added that that this strategy is still just beginning.

"This is the start of a program. And players can expect a reliable pipeline that validates their historical investment in the Xbox platform, keeps them as Xbox players going forward, and everyone in the industry understands that exclusives are important to the growth and branding of that platform," said Ball.

Everything that Sharma, Ball, and the rest of the new leadership suite is doing now seems to be getting the stamp of approval from Xbox fans, but it'll be interesting to see if it actually does pave a path towards Xbox hardware being popular again and platform exclusives pulling people towards Xbox the way it used to, two console generations ago.

About the author: David has been writing about videogames, technology, and culture since 2020, with a focus on reporting daily news across multiple publications, including GameDaily.Biz, GameSkinny, and PlayStation Universe before joining Wccftech in 2025. David started contributing as Canada/US reporter for Wccftech's gaming section in 2025. Besides being up-to-date on the industry's movements, he loves interviewing developers, reviewing games, and writing intricate essays about the symbolism and layered meanings to be found in rich narratives as he's done for publications like GamesIndustry.Biz, LostInCult, and others. Outside of games he loves movies, music, theatre, his hometown, and his family, though not necessarily in that order.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/xbox-game-pass-lost-millions-subscribers-with-price-hike/

item.title: Valheim 1.0 launches September 9th for PC and consoles
item.content:encoded:
Iron Gate has unveiled the 1.0 release date for its Viking inspired survival game Valheim, with the game launching September 9th. It will launching for PC, Mac, Linux, Xbox One, Xbox Series XS, PS5, and Switch 2. The latter two platforms have just been confirmed alongside the release date announcement, and you can check out the trailer below.

Valheim 1.0 will have crossplay between all of the platforms so up to 10 players can work together to explore, build settlements, and fight monsters. Valheim originally launched back in February 2021 and quickly went on to become one of the biggest breakout hits of the year. Its lo-fi visuals, Viking theme, and refined survival gameplay created the perfect storm, no doubt fuelled by the increasing boredom of gamers stuck in lockdown. Over ten million copies were sold in the game’s first year.

Valheim Early Access Preview – A survival game with a sense of purpose

In our Valheim Early Access preview, Thomas said, “Valheim, while a bit rough around the edges, has a number of genuinely nice touches to its gameplay. When cooking food, you have to actually stand by it as it cooks and take it off when it’s ready – leave it on for too long and you’ll be left with a piece of coal – but stand by a fire in a cramped space and you’ll start to die due to smoke inhalation. For a game that’s just entered Early Access, Valheim already feels like a mostly complete game. The future feels bright for Valheim. With a solid arrangement of survival systems already in place, an engaging array of boss battles and plenty of extra touches, I can’t wait to see how the community shapes and guides this survival sim’s future development. This is definitely one to watch out for.”

Source: Steam



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/06/08/valheim-1-0-launches-september-9th-for-pc-and-consoles/

item.title: Gears of War: E-Day - Open Beta Start Dates and How To Get Early Access
item.content:encoded:
Gears of War: E-Day's big showing at the Xbox Games Showcase was primarily focused on its campaign, but the game is also set to have what will undoubtedly be a killer multiplayer mode too, and players won't have to wait too long to try it.

Xbox has confirmed that a multiplayer beta is coming, starting on August 6. While the "open beta" will eventually be available to everyone, those who preorder Gears of War: E-Day can get in early and play during the first beta weekend.

Here are all the details you need until then.

Gears of War: E-Day

As far as Beta Codes go, the Gears website also says that, "Players who digitally pre-order Gears of War: E-Day are automatically granted Early Access to the Open Beta and do not require a Beta Code."

Players who pre-order a physical edition should expect to receive a Beta Code at the point of sale from participating retailers, granting entry into Open Beta for Early Access."

That means Gears of War: E-Day's beta is ultimately open to everyone, but, as we mentioned, players who preorder any edition of the game can jump in before the wider public (that includes the standard, premium, or collector's edition).

Xbox Game Pass and PC Game Pass subscribers will also receive access during the August 6 weekend as part of their membership benefits.

As far as how it works for everyone else looking to access in the second beta weekend, we'll have to await further information from Xbox.

When is the Gears of War: E-Day Open Beta?

Xbox has confirmed that Gears of War: E-Day's multiplayer beta will be split across two weekends, starting on August 6.

Gears of War E-Day is an Xbox Console Exclusive

Standard Edition of Gears of War: E-Day is available in both a physical and digital format, and listed for $69.99. But, it's only available for Xbox consoles and on PC. This one isn't coming to PS5.

Alongside the main game and getting early access to the Open Beta, ordering now also sets you up with a preorder bonus of a character skin and weapon skin.

Gears of War: E-Day Gameplay Demo Reveal Xbox Games Showcase 2026 - Screenshots

If you are going this route, I'd suggest a choosing retailer like Best Buy or Amazon, which won't charge you until the game eventually ships in October later this year.

If you don't click with the game during the beta, you can simply cancel your preorder without the stress of having to await a refund.

Robert Anderson is IGN's Senior Commerce Editor and resident deals expert on games, collectibles, trading card games, and more. You can follow him @robertliam21 on Bluesky.



https://www.ign.com/articles/gears-of-war-e-day-open-beta-how-to-play

item.title: Halo: Campaign Evolved Preorders Are Live for PS5 and Xbox – Here’s What Comes in Each Edition
item.content:encoded:
Halo: Campaign Evolved Preorders Are Live for PS5 and Xbox – Here’s What Comes in Each Edition

Releasing very soon on July 28.

Good news, Halo fans: Halo: Campaign Evolved will be released next month on July 28. If you simply can't wait to spend your summer days absorbed in this remake, preorders are already live, so you can secure a copy today.

A Standard Edition, Premium Edition, and Collector's Edition are all available to check out right now, and it's not just Xbox users that get to drop some cash on this release. The game is also coming to PlayStation 5, alongside PC, so everyone can step into Master Chief’s shoes this summer.

Below you can learn more about each edition, what they're priced at, and what they come with.

Halo: Campaign Evolved – Standard Edition

Halo: Campaign Evolved – Premium Edition

Halo: Campaign Evolved Premium Edition - Xbox Series X, Xbox Series S, Windows [Digital]

Digital

  • See at Best Buy (Xbox Series X/S) - $69.99
  • See at Xbox - $69.99
  • See at PlayStation - $69.99

PC

  • See at Steam - $69.99

Halo: Campaign Evolved's Premium Edition, which appears to only be available as a digital version, is priced at $69.99. This comes with quite a few bonuses alongside the full game and preorder bonus, including:

  • Up to five days of early access beginning July 23
  • The Alpha Halo Armory Pack
  • A Digital Story & Art Collection that includes Halo: Hungry Buzzards - Per Xbox Wire, it's "a new digital short story by Troy Denning that leads into the events of Operation: METEORITE"
  • The Art of Halo: Campaign Evolved digital artbook
  • A digital game manual inspired by the original 2001 Halo: Combat Evolved game manual

Below, you can get a closer look at the Masher Chief skins and weapon skins included in the Alpha Halo Armory Pack.

Halo: Campaign Evolved – Collector’s Edition

  • See at Xbox Game Studios Shop - $199.99 (CURRENTLY OUT OF STOCK)

The Halo: Campaign Evolved Collector's Edition looks like quite the pick-up. For $199.99, you get everything in the Premium Edition alongside:

  • 12-inch Dark Horse Master Chief statue
  • Light-up LED Cortana chip
  • A Steelbook
  • Three original concept art prints
  • Physical version of the reimagined game manual
  • A game disc for Xbox Series X or PlayStation 5 consoles (the Steam version includes a code only)

Halo: Campaign Evolved Preorder Bonus

Any edition of Halo: Campaign Evolved that you preorder will come with a bonus of the Foundry Armory Pack, which you can get a closer look at in the image above. This comes with:

  • Classic 2001 Mark V Armor skin
  • Classic 2001 Assault Rifle skin
  • Gilded Onyx Armor style
  • Gilded Onyx Assault Rifle style

Halo: Campaign Evolved is an Unreal Engine 5 remake of 2001's Halo: Combat Evolved. It features the original 10-mission campaign alongside something new that was revealed during the most recent Xbox Games Showcase: Operation: METEORITE.

According to an Xbox Wire post from Brian Jarrard, Community Director at Halo Studios, "Included with every edition of Halo: Campaign Evolved, Operation: METEORITE is a new three-mission story arc that takes place a year before the events of the first game. Featuring the Master Chief and Sgt. Johnson, this new adventure sends players behind enemy lines in a clandestine UNSC operation aboard a Covenant research vessel."

IGN's Ryan McCaffrey was also able to play a bit of Halo: Campaign Evolved last year and wrote in a preview, "I’m still left with many unanswered questions about this remake project. But to Halo Studios' credit, this early demo did answer a couple of key ones about how good it could look and how Halo-y it could feel."
